response.justification: While Sarah demonstrates exceptional software engineering fundamentals and 16+ years of professional experience, the resume reveals critical misalignments with this embedded networking security role. Most significantly, there is no evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency (a hard requirement), and her entire career has focused on web/backend systems rather than embedded firmware development. The limited C programming experience (last used 2014-2015) and complete absence of security technologies expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption systems) and networking stack knowledge (Layer-2/3 protocols, switch operation, NETCONF/SNMP) represent substantial domain gaps requiring extensive retraining.
strengths: 16+ years of software development experience across diverse domains demonstrates strong fundamentals and ability to learn new technologies
Proven track record with Linux systems, backend infrastructure, and some systems-level programming at National Instruments
Strong teaching background and knowledge-sharing initiatives indicate deep technical understanding and communication skills valuable for cross-team collaboration
concerns: No mention of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is a required qualification for this role
Entire career focused on web/backend and application development; zero embedded systems, firmware development, or network switch product experience

C programming experience is minimal and dated (2014-2015); completely absent security technologies (encryption, key exchange, authentication systems) and networking expertise (Layer-2/3, NETCONF, SNMP, switch operation, Telemetry)

key_factors.factor: No Embedded Firmware or Security Domain Experience
Minimal Recent C Programming and Linux Kernel Knowledge
